cards.back: ∠H(ω) = −ωt_d (linear through origin). All frequencies delayed by same t_d. Output = x(t − t_d).
Non-linear phase → different frequencies get different time delays → signal shape changes even if magnitudes preserved.
H(ω) = P_{2B}(ω)·e^{−jωt_d}. Rectangular magnitude (|H|=1 for |ω|<B) plus linear phase (∠H=−ωt_d).

cards.front: Constant phase ∠H = C: time delay at frequency ω?
cards.back: Delay = C/ω. Different for each ω → signal distortion. Not the same as linear phase.

cards.front: Why does flat magnitude alone not guarantee distortionless?
cards.back: Flat |H| prevents amplitude distortion but doesn't prevent phase distortion. Need BOTH flat magnitude AND linear phase.
Propagation delay (group delay). Constant for linear phase. Units: seconds. t_d = −d(∠H)/dω.

cards.front: Two-component signal through linear-phase filter: y(t) = ?
cards.back: y(t) = A₀cos(ω₀(t−t_d)) + A₁cos(ω₁(t−t_d)). Both delayed by same t_d → no shape change.

cards.front: Two-component signal through constant-phase filter: y(t) = ?
cards.back: y(t) = A₀cos(ω₀t−C) + A₁cos(ω₁t−C). Delays are C/ω₀ ≠ C/ω₁ → relative phase shift → distortion.
Rectangular pulse: P_{2B}(ω) = 1 for |ω| ≤ B, 0 for |ω| > B. Width = 2B, centred at ω = 0.

cards.front: If ∠H(ω) = −5ω, what is t_d and what is y(t)?
cards.back: t_d = 5 seconds. Output y(t) = x(t − 5). Pure delay, no distortion.

cards.front: Group delay definition for linear phase?
cards.back: τ_g = −d(∠H)/dω. For ∠H = −ωt_d: τ_g = t_d (constant). Constant group delay = no distortion.

cards.front: What two conditions define a 'distortionless' filter?
cards.back: 1) |H(ω)| = constant (usually 1) in passband. 2) ∠H(ω) = −ωt_d (linear phase). Both needed simultaneously.
